plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here is my complete implementation plan for the `@ai-sdk/pixelart` provider. The plan file has been written to `/Users/timpietrusky/.claude/plans/kind-weaving-barto.md`.

---

## Plan Summary

### What gets created: 17 files total, 1 existing file modified

**Closest reference:** `packages/black-forest-labs` — the only other image-only provider in the repo; the PixelArt package mirrors its structure exactly.

---

### Source files (`packages/pixelart/src/`)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `version.ts` | Build-time version constant (same pattern everywhere) |
| `pixelart-image-settings.ts` | `PixelArtImageModelId` type: `'pixel-art-v1' \| 'pixel-art-v2' \| 'retro-art-v1' \| (string & {})` |
| `pixelart-image-model.ts` | `PixelArtImageModel implements ImageModelV3` — POST to `/images/generations`, parses base64 response, warns on `aspectRatio`, validates `providerOptions.pixelArt` (`style: 'pixel'\|'retro'\|'8bit'`, `pixelDensity: number`) via Zod + `parseProviderOptions` |
| `pixelart-provider.ts` | `createPixelArt()` factory + `pixelArt` default; env var `PIXELART_API_KEY`; `Bearer` auth; `NoSuchModelError` for language/embedding |
| `index.ts` | Public exports |
| `pixelart-image-model.test.ts` | Vitest tests via `createTestServer` |
| `pixelart-provider.test.ts` | Factory/config tests via `vi.mock` |

### Config files (`packages/pixelart/`)

`package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.build.json`, `tsup.config.ts`, `vitest.node.config.js`, `vitest.edge.config.js`, `turbo.json` — all copied verbatim from `black-forest-labs` with names/versions changed.

### Other files

- **Root `tsconfig.json`** — add `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` alphabetically between `perplexity` and `provider`
- **`.changeset/<slug>.md`** — `major` changeset for the new package
- **`examples/ai-functions/src/generate-image/pixelart/basic.ts`** — runnable usage example

### Fictional API design (synchronous, no polling)

```
POST https://api.pixelart.ai/v1/images/generations
Body: { prompt, n, size, seed, style, pixel_density, response_format: "b64_json" }
Response: { created, data: [{ b64_json, revised_prompt }] }
```
